title: Machine-learned interatomic potential for predictive simulation of $\mathrm{Mo}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ epitaxy
abstract:
Author(s): Emir Bilgili, Nicholas Taormina, Richard Hennig, Simon R. Phillpot, and Youping Chen
A machine-learned interatomic potential (MLIP) for multilayer $\mathrm{Mo}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ was developed using the ultrafast force field (UF3) framework. The UF3 MLIP reproduces key properties in strong agreement with DFT including lattice constants, interlayer binding energies, and phase stability…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 054002] Published Tue May 19, 2026
relevance_reason: Develops MLIP (UF3) for MoS2, validated against DFT; all criteria satisfied.

title: Active-learned potentials reveal the interplay between the structure and dynamics in thorium molten salts
abstract:
Author(s): Vitor Ferreira Grizzi, Benjamin Nebgen, and Y Z
The introduction of high-valence actinides, such as thorium and uranium, into molten alkali halide induces complex structural correlations and intermediate-range ordering, presenting a daunting challenge for computational materials science. Because these dynamic topological features span length scal…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 065606] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026
relevance_reason: Active-learned interatomic potentials for thorium molten salts; explicit atoms, MLIP development and validation; satisfies (a)-(c).

title: DFT investigation of gas adsorption and electronic modulation on Cd- and Zn-decorated PdSe2 monolayers for sensor applications to NO, CO, and NO2
abstract:

title: Hydrogen trapping and passivation of intrinsic acceptor defects in the ${\mathrm{CuInSe}}_{2}$ chalcopyrite
abstract:
Author(s): A. G. Marinopoulos and R. C. Vilão
Hydrogen is an element frequently found within the lattice of the ${\mathrm{CuInSe}}_{2}$ (CIS) chalcopyrite and its alloys in solar-cell devices, in many occasions introduced intentionally to passivate the electrical activity of existing defect centers. The present study reports first-principles ca…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 054603] Published Tue May 19, 2026
relevance_reason: First-principles DFT study of defects in CuInSe2; all criteria satisfied.
